title: Renewable Variability & Electricity Price Risk
text:
This case study quantifies how wind and solar generation variability drives day-ahead electricity price risk in Germany and the Nordic bidding zones. It is structured in four stages:

Weather → Generation — ERA5 wind and irradiance to capacity factor models
Price Decomposition — attributing price variance to its physical drivers

Risk Quantification — VaR and CVaR for a stylised renewable portfolio
Policy Implications — connecting findings to the ENTSO-E hedging framework

Data sources: SMARD (Bundesnetzagentur, CC BY 4.0), ENTSO-E Transparency Platform, Copernicus ERA5 reanalysis.
